content.text:
{
  "module": "project-intelligence",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 62,
    "domain_depth": 58,
    "data_richness": 54,
    "interaction_quality": 55,
    "ai_integration": 61,
    "mobile_first": 52,
    "performance": 63,
    "typescript_quality": 67,
    "error_handling": 56,
    "realtime": 65,
    "accessibility": 48,
    "security": 71,
    "differentiation": 59,
    "enterprise_readiness": 68
  },
  "overall_score": 60,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "aiHealthScore and aiConfidenceLevel are hardcoded placeholders",
      "description": "In src/api/endpoints/projects.ts getMetrics(), aiHealthScore is hardcoded to 85 and aiConfidenceLevel to 74. CFOs and PMs see fake numbers on the primary dashboard. This destroys trust and violates the founder's explicit rule: every metric must be a real calculated number.",
      "prompt": "File: src/api/endpoints/projects.ts, function: getMetrics(). Remove hardcoded aiHealthScore: 85 and aiConfidenceLevel: 74. Instead, compute aiHealthScore as a weighted composite: (scheduleHealth * 0.35) + (budgetHealth * 0.35) + (rfiHealth * 0.20) + (safetyHealth * 0.10), where each component = 100 * (1 - Math.min(1, negativeIndicator/threshold)). Compute aiConfidenceLevel from data completeness: percentage of metric fields returned from project_metrics that are non-null, clamped 0-100. Pull these values from the already-fetched project_metrics row. Add types for both fields in src/types/api.ts ProjectMetrics interface. Acceptance criteria: (1) aiHealthScore changes when rfi_overdue or budget_spent changes in the DB, (2) aiConfidenceLevel is 100 when all metric fields are populated and drops proportionally when fields are null, (3) no hardcoded numeric literals remain in getMetrics()."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Change order approved_cost always zero — projected final cost is wrong",
      "description": "In src/api/endpoints/budget.ts getCostData(), approved_cost is always set to 0 for every change order regardless of status. This makes computeProjectFinancials() return a systematically incorrect Projected Final Cost and Variance, breaking the CFO dashboard and AIA billing accuracy.",
      "prompt": "File: src/api/endpoints/budget.ts, function: getCostData(). Fix the MappedChangeOrder mapping: set approved_cost to co.amount when co.status === 'approved', else 0. Also verify the ChangeOrderRow type in src/types/api.ts includes an approved_amount column; if the DB stores it separately, use that field. Update computeProjectFinancials() in src/lib/financialEngine.ts to sum approved_cost from change orders with status 'approved' for Revised Contract Value, and sum amount for change orders with status 'pending' for Pending Exposure. Add a unit test in src/test covering a mixed array of approved/pending/draft COs and assert Revised Contract Value = Original + sum(approved only). Acceptance criteria: (1) approved_cost > 0 for any CO with status 'approved', (2) financialEngine Revised Contract Value excludes draft and pending COs, (3) test passes."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "security",
      "title": "getEntityHistory has no project-scope guard — cross-project data leakage",
      "description": "In src/api/endpoints/auditTrail.ts, getEntityHistory() queries the audit_log table by entityType and entityId only — no project_id or organization_id filter. Any authenticated user can call this function with any entity ID and read audit entries from other organizations' projects.",
      "prompt": "File: src/api/endpoints/auditTrail.ts, function: getEntityHistory(). Add a required projectId: string parameter as the first argument. Add .eq('project_id', projectId) to the Supabase query before the entityType and entityId filters. Import validateProjectId from src/api/middleware/projectScope.ts and call it at the top of the function. Update all callers of getEntityHistory() across the codebase to pass projectId. Also verify that the audit_log table in Supabase has a Row Level Security policy that enforces project membership — if not, add a migration note in src/lib/rls.ts. Acceptance criteria: (1) calling getEntityHistory with a valid entityId but a projectId the user does not belong to returns empty array or throws a 403 ApiError, (2) TypeScript signature requires projectId, (3) all existing callers compile without error."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-004",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Silent AI fallback swallows errors with no user feedback",
      "description": "In src/api/endpoints/ai.ts getAiInsights(), when aiService.generateInsights() throws, the error is caught and execution falls through to Supabase silently. The user sees stale DB insights with no indication the live AI failed. On the PM dashboard this means outdated risk alerts display as if current.",
      "prompt": "File: src/api/endpoints/ai.ts, function: getAiInsights(). Change the catch block to: (1) log the error to src/lib/errorTracking.ts captureException(), (2) set a flag on the returned insights array — add a metadata field source: 'cached' and a generatedAt timestamp from the Supabase row, (3) export a new getAiInsightsMeta() that returns { live: boolean, lastUpdated: string } so the UI can show a banner like 'AI insights updated 2 hours ago — live analysis unavailable'. Update src/types/ai.ts AIInsight to add optional source: 'live' | 'cached' | 'supabase'. In the AI insights panel component (src/components/dashboard/widgets/AIInsightsWidget.tsx), render an amber banner when any insight has source !== 'live'. Acceptance criteria: (1) error is captured in errorTracking, (2) AIInsightsWidget shows staleness banner when source is cached, (3) no silent swallowing of errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C2-005",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "typescript_quality",
      "title": "Widespread use of any casts in organizations.ts breaks type safety",
      "description": "In src/api/endpoints/organizations.ts, three locations use explicit any[]: the organization_members join is cast as any[], getOrganizationProjects uses any for project fields like p.total_value and p.status. This bypasses TypeScript protection on financial aggregations used in the CFO portfolio view.",
      "prompt": "File: src/api/endpoints/organizations.ts. Replace all any casts with proper types. (1) Import OrganizationMemberRow and ProjectRow from src/types/api.ts. (2) For the organization_members join, define interface OrgMemberWithOrg { organization: Organization } and use it instead of any[]. (3) In getPortfolioMetrics(), replace all p.status, p.total_value, p.budget_spent, p.completion_percentage, p.schedule_variance_days accesses with typed ProjectRow field access — verify these column names exist in src/types/database.ts and align them. (4) Enable strict: true in tsconfig if not already set and fix any resulting errors in this file. Acceptance criteria: (1) zero any[] usages in organizations.ts, (2) tsc --noEmit passes on this file, (3) portfolio metrics still compute correctly."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C2-006",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Lien waiver workflow has no API endpoint or data model",
      "description": "The founder's P0 list and competitive analysis both flag lien waiver tracking as a critical missing workflow. No endpoint exists in src/api/endpoints/ for lien waivers, and the directory structure shows only src/components/export/LienWaiverPDF.tsx — a PDF template with no backing data layer.",
      "prompt": "Create src/api/endpoints/lienWaivers.ts. Define interface LienWaiver { id, project_id, subcontractor_id, payment_period, type: 'conditional_progress' | 'unconditional_progress' | 'conditional_final' | 'unconditional_final', amount, status: 'pending' | 'received' | 'missing', payment_app_id, created_at, received_at }. Implement: getLienWaivers(projectId) — queries lien_waivers table with project_id filter and validateProjectId; createLienWaiver(projectId, data) — inserts and returns new record; markReceived(waiverId, receivedAt) — updates status and received_at. Add getLienWaiverSummary(projectId) that returns { total, received, missing, missingSubcontractors: string[] }. Register query keys in src/api/queryKeys.ts. Add the lien_waivers table definition to src/types/database.ts. Acceptance criteria: (1) getLienWaivers returns typed LienWaiver[], (2) getLienWaiverSummary correctly counts missing vs received, (3) all functions have projectId validation."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C2-007",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Daily log maps workers_onsite and total_hours but field app has no offline mutation path",
      "description": "src/api/endpoints/field.ts getDailyLogs() correctly maps field data, but there is no createDailyLog or updateDailyLog mutation endpoint with offline queue support. Superintendents submitting daily logs at 4pm on a spotty LTE connection will lose data. src/services/offlineQueue.ts exists but is not wired to daily log mutations.",
      "prompt": "File: src/api/endpoints/field.ts. Add createDailyLog(projectId, payload: DailyLogPayload) and updateDailyLog(id, payload) functions using supabaseMutation from src/api/client.ts. Define DailyLogPayload type in src/types/api.ts with fields: log_date, weather, workers_onsite, total_hours, incidents, summary, photos string[]. In src/hooks/useOfflineMutation.ts (or create it if absent), wrap createDailyLog so it enqueues to src/services/offlineQueue.ts when navigator.onLine is false, using the existing OfflineQueueItem pattern. Add a useDailyLogMutation() hook in src/hooks/mutations/index.ts that uses this offline-aware wrapper. Show a sync-pending badge in the daily log UI (src/components/dailylog/QuickEntry.tsx) when items are queued. Acceptance criteria: (1) createDailyLog queues when offline and syncs on reconnect, (2) QuickEntry shows pending badge, (3) no data loss on flaky connections."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C2-008",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "ai_integration",
      "title": "AI copilot has no construction-context system prompt or project data injection",
      "description": "src/api/endpoints/aiService.ts delegates to aiService.draftRFIResponse and summarizeDailyLog but inspection of src/lib/aiService.ts shows no evidence that the system prompt includes project financials, schedule state, or open RFI count. The AI cannot be the 'smartest project engineer' without project context.",
      "prompt": "File: src/lib/aiService.ts and src/lib/aiPrompts.ts. Build a buildProjectContext(projectId) async function in aiPrompts.ts that fetches: (1) project name, contract value, phase from projects table, (2) open and overdue RFI counts, (3) budget variance by top 3 CSI divisions, (4) next 3 schedule milestones, (5) last 3 daily log summaries. Serialize this into a structured string. In aiService.ts, prepend this context to every system prompt sent to the LLM. Add a CONSTRUCTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT constant that establishes the AI as a senior project engineer — include terminology: RFI, submittal, change order, retainage, CSI, AIA G702. Cache the context object in src/hooks/useProjectCache.ts with a 5-minute TTL. Acceptance criteria: (1) every AI call includes project-specific context, (2) draftRFIResponse references the specific RFI's drawing and spec section, (3) context fetch adds under 200ms via caching."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C3-009",
      "severity": "medium",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "No ARIA roles or keyboard navigation on metric cards or data tables",
      "description": "The design standards require WCAG 2.1 AA compliance including keyboard navigation (J/K for table rows, Enter to open). src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx exists but src/hooks/useTableKeyboardNavigation.ts is defined without evidence it is wired to the primary project intelligence tables for RFIs, budget, and schedule.",
      "prompt": "File: src/hooks/useTableKeyboardNavigation.ts and src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx. Verify useTableKeyboardNavigation implements: J/K arrow navigation between rows, Enter to trigger onRowClick, Space to toggle row selection. Ensure the hook sets aria-activedescendant on the table container and each row has a unique id attribute. In DataTable.tsx, apply role='grid' to the table element, role='row' to each tr, role='gridcell' to each td, and role='columnheader' to each th with aria-sort when sortable. Add aria-label to all icon-only buttons in table action columns. Wire useTableKeyboardNavigation to the RFIs table (src/pages/RFIs.tsx), Budget table (src/pages/Budget.tsx), and Schedule table (src/pages/Schedule.tsx). Acceptance criteria: (1) axe-core reports zero critical violations on these three pages, (2) full table traversal possible with keyboard only, (3) screen reader announces row content on focus."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C3-010",
      "severity": "medium",
      "dimension": "performance",
      "title": "getPortfolioMetrics fetches all project rows then filters in JS — N+1 at scale",
      "description": "In src/api/endpoints/organizations.ts getPortfolioMetrics(), all projects are fetched then filtered client-side (activeProjects, projectsOnSchedule). For a GC with 50 projects this fetches thousands of rows and does JS aggregation that should be a DB aggregate. This will be slow for the CFO portfolio view.",
      "prompt": "File: src/api/endpoints/organizations.ts, function: getPortfolioMetrics(). Replace the two-step fetch-then-filter pattern with Supabase RPC calls. Create a Postgres function get_portfolio_metrics(org_id uuid) that returns a single row with all aggregated fields: total_projects, active_projects (count where status='active'), total_contract_value (sum of contract_value), avg_completion_percentage, projects_on_schedule (count where schedule_variance_days >= 0), projects_at_risk (count where schedule_variance_days < -7). Call it via supabase.rpc('get_portfolio_metrics', { org_id: orgId }). Keep the separate RFI and punch item parallel queries but add .eq('status', 'active') scoping where possible. Add the RPC type signature to src/types/database.ts under Database['public']['Functions']. Acceptance criteria: (1) getPortfolioMetrics makes at most 3 DB round-trips regardless of project count, (2) result is identical to current implementation for same data, (3) p95 latency under 300ms for 100-project org."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "name": "Predictive Delay Engine — Monday Morning Brief",
      "description": "Every Monday at 6 AM, an overnight job reads the schedule baseline vs actual, open RFI log, weather forecast API, and pending submittals with lead times. It computes which activities have float <= 3 days AND a blocking condition (overdue RFI, unapproved submittal, rain forecast). It generates a ranked list of 'This week's risks' with AI-written plain-English summaries and pushes them as a prioritized notification to the superintendent's phone and the PM's dashboard widget. This is the 'AI that catches problems before they become delays' — the #1 north star feature from the vision document.",
      "implementation_hint": "src/lib/predictions.ts already exists — extend it with a weeklyBriefEngine(projectId) function. Schedule via a Supabase Edge Function cron. Store output in ai_insights table with type='predictive_delay' and expires_at = next Monday. Surface in AIInsightsWidget with a distinct amber banner style."
    },
    {
      "name": "Subcontractor Scorecard — Portfolio-Wide",
      "description": "A cross-project subcontractor performance dashboard that aggregates: on-time delivery rate (daily log material receipts vs promised dates), RFI response time (average days from assignment to response), quality score (punch items created per $1M of subcontract value), safety incidents (from daily log incident fields), and submittal approval rate (approved vs revise-and-resubmit ratio). Displayed as a sortable table on the Crews/Directory page with color-coded performance bands. A GC executive can instantly see which electrical sub is underperforming across all 20 of their active projects.",
      "implementation_hint": "New endpoint src/api/endpoints/subcontractorScorecard.ts. Query across projects using organizationId scope. Compute all metrics server-side in a Postgres function. Display in src/pages/Crews.tsx with a toggle between 'This Project' and 'Portfolio View'."
    },
    {
      "name": "13-Week Cash Flow Engine",
      "description": "A CFO-grade forward-looking cash flow projection built from real data: approved pay apps (inflows, timed by historical collection speed per owner), approved subcontract invoices due (outflows, timed by pay-when-paid clauses), retainage release schedule (projected from substantial completion date), and pending change orders (modeled as probability-weighted inflows). Displayed as a bar+line chart showing weekly net position and cumulative cash balance. Exportable to Excel. Flags any week where projected balance goes negative.",
      "implementation_hint": "src/lib/financialEngine.ts already has computeProjectFinancials — add computeCashFlowProjection(projectId, weeks=13). New widget src/components/dashboard/widgets/CashFlowWidget.tsx already exists in directory — wire it to real data instead of mock. Add 13-week projection schema to src/schemas/forecast.ts."
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "SiteSyncAI's Project Intelligence module has solid architectural foundations — typed API layer, financial engine separation, state machines for workflow entities, and an AI service abstraction — that Procore and ACC lack in their monolithic designs. However, the module falls short of the vision in three critical areas: (1) key metrics are hardcoded placeholders that a CFO would immediately distrust, (2) the lien waiver workflow is a PDF template with no data layer, exactly the 'bolted on' criticism leveled at Procore, and (3) the AI copilot lacks construction-context injection, making it functionally equivalent to a generic chatbot rather than the domain-expert engineer the vision demands. Fieldwire beats SiteSync on offline field mutation reliability today — the missing offline daily log mutation path is a superintendent-level failure. Fix these five critical/high issues and SiteSync's technical architecture will be genuinely ahead of Procore's module fragmentation and ACC's field-weak UX.",
  "summary": "The Project Intelligence module scores 60/100 — a platform with strong structural bones undermined by critical data integrity gaps and missing construction-domain features. The API layer is well-organized with proper scoping middleware and a financial engine that correctly models CSI divisions and change order states. However, hardcoded health scores and a systematic approved_cost=0 bug make every financial metric on the CFO dashboard untrustworthy. The lien waiver workflow — a P0 founder priority and a daily GC pain point — has a PDF export component but zero data layer. The AI copilot delegates to an aiService abstraction without injecting project context, making it a construction-branded wrapper around a generic LLM. The audit trail has a security gap allowing cross-project entity history reads. Fix the five critical and high issues — hardcoded metrics, approved_cost, cross-project audit leak, AI context injection, and lien waiver data model — and this module will be in the top tier of construction PM platforms. The invented features (Predictive Delay Engine, Portfolio Subcontractor Scorecard, 13-Week Cash Flow Engine) are the category-defining capabilities that would make a $500M GC switch overnight."
}
